other_observers: I observed the bird with David Leatherman.; I am not the original finder. Nick Komar was the original finder, and several others saw it afterward including Cade Cropper. Other than these individuals, I don't know the others who saw it.
description: The entire time that I watched the bird, it was floating with a group of Bonaparte's Gulls. The Little Gull was noticeably smaller than the surrounding Bonaparte's Gulls. The Little Gull's bill was entirely black. The top of its head had a decent amount of black coloring on it, unlike the Bonaparte's Gulls. The Little Gull had a dark eye with some dark feathering immediately adjacent to the eye. It had a decent amount of white on its face, except for the black spot behind its eye. The back of the Little Gull was mostly gray, but the folded wings were very black, especially the carpal area. The carpal was much darker and striking than the Bonaparte's Gulls'. The Little Gull's primaries were black. I just saw a little bit of the body under the tail and it was white. The sides of the bird were white and under the chin was white.Behaviors: Floating with other Bonaparte's Gulls. This is apparently a common practice of Little Gulls.Call: No call or song heardPlumage: Firts winter
